intro: Microcos blattaefolia, also known as the "stinking chamomile," is a perennial herb native to Southeast Asia, belonging to the family Malvaceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, thanks to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The bioactive constituents of Microcos blattaefolia include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is also used to make topical ointments and salves, allowing for its bioactive compounds to be absorbed through the skin.
